name: Gyeongsangbuk-do (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity and the heritage of the Silla kingdom whose ancient capital Gyeongju lies in the province
the East Sea, which forms the eastern boundary of the province
the Sobaek and Taebaek mountain ranges and the province's forests
vitality, the warmth of the people, and the spirit of the historic kingdom

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem in blue, green and red, depicting an abstract sun, mountains and waves, with the wordmark 'GYEONGBUK' below.
history: North Gyeongsang Province (Gyeongsangbuk-do, also known as Gyeongbuk) traces its identity to the ancient Silla kingdom (57 BC – 935 AD), whose capital Gyeongju lies within its borders. The current provincial flag dates from a redesign in the 2010s. The emblem combines abstracted forms representing the Taebaek and Sobaek mountains, the East Sea, and the rising sun, expressing the province's natural setting and historical continuity.

features.symbolism: abstract motif of mountain ranges, sun and waves; the design represents the Taebaek and Sobaek ranges, the East Sea, and Gyeongbuk's historic identity as the heart of the ancient Silla kingdom
